sourceMovie.title: The Smashing Machine
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: The Smashing Machine (2025) stuck because it commits to its drama premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Benny Safdie's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The drama and history blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Braveheart
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Smashing Machine clicked for you, Braveheart (1995) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and history, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Smashing Machine?
faq.answer: Try Braveheart, Raging Bull, Hotel Rwanda—each shares DNA with The Smashing Machine while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Smashing Machine?
faq.answer: The Smashing Machine (2025) is best described as Drama, History. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Smashing Machine worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
